responses.global_analysis: 1. JSON Match: Used the provided Reference JSON to help structure the lyric transitions and spelling of 'Special Generation', but completely discarded it for the talk/merchandise section. 2. JSON Usage: The reference JSON served as a great guide for the song's key terms, while the latter half of the video was fully transcribed manually as the reference JSON was null. 3. Timing & Pacing: Strictly aligned start timecodes with Kiara's vocal delivery, adding precise cuts for seamless Bilingual translation.

subtitles.original: 誰しも 彼しも 驚くような

subtitles.original: Bye-bye!
subtitles.english: Bye-bye!

subtitles.original: One more! Ka-pow!
subtitles.english: One more! Ka-pow!

subtitles.original: Bye-bye!
subtitles.english: Bye-bye!

subtitles.original: 告知、告知！
subtitles.english: Announcement, announcement!

subtitles.original: 小鳥遊キアラ誕生日記念グッズ2026！
subtitles.english: Takanashi Kiara Birthday Merchandise 2026!

subtitles.original: こちらがバナーです。
subtitles.english: Here is the banner.

subtitles.original: まず第一に、メインキャラクターとなる大きなアイテムは、抱き枕です！
subtitles.english: First of all, the big item that is the main character is... body pillow!

subtitles.original: 何年もグッズを作ってきて、ついにやりました。
subtitles.english: I finally did it after so many years of doing merchandise.

subtitles.original: ちょっとネタ切れになってきたのかもしれないので…
subtitles.english: I might be running out of ideas a little bit, so...

subtitles.original: 元々は絶対にやらないだろうなと思っていたことをやりました。
subtitles.english: I did something that originally I never thought I would do.

subtitles.original: KFP、私を放っておかないで。ベッドに寝かせておいてね、イェーイ！
subtitles.english: KFP, don't leave me hanging. Leave me laying on the bed, yeah!

subtitles.original: 1つ目のアイテムはなんと！一生やらないと思ってたんですけど、
subtitles.english: The first item is... a body pillow cover! I thought I'd never do this...

subtitles.original: やっぱりここまで活動すると、もう出すしかなかった。抱き枕です！
subtitles.english: but after being active for this long, I had to do it!

subtitles.original: イェーイ！
subtitles.english: Yeah!

subtitles.original: 次はとっても可愛いぬいぐるみセットです
subtitles.english: Next, we have a really cute plushie set

subtitles.original: 私の愛猫たち、スムージーとチョンカーズ！
subtitles.english: of my kitty cat babies! Smoothie and Chonkers!

subtitles.original: とってもキュートでふわふわ、どこにでも連れて行けます
subtitles.english: They're so cute and fluffy, and I can bring them everywhere now,

subtitles.original: みんなもバッグや鍵につけることができますよ。
subtitles.english: and you should too. You can hang them on your bag or keys.

subtitles.original: 2つ目のアイテムはそうですね、私の猫たちのぬいぐるみセットで、
subtitles.english: The second item is a plushie set of my cats,

subtitles.original: キーチェーンなどとして使えます。
subtitles.english: which you can use as keychains.

subtitles.original: え、どこにでも連れてってあげてください。
subtitles.english: Please take them with you wherever you go.

subtitles.original: 次はこちらの缶バッジですが、今回は四角形なので、ちょっと珍しいです…
subtitles.english: Next one is these badges, but they are a bit unusual because this time they are square...

subtitles.original: …そしてフェイクレザー製なので、少し柔らかいです。
subtitles.english: ...and made from fake leather, so they are a bit squishy.

subtitles.original: もちろん、これを使って痛バッグを作ることもできます。
subtitles.english: And of course you can make an ita-bag with this, too.

subtitles.original: 今回のバッジはなんと、フェイクレザーで出来てますし、あと四角だし、
subtitles.english: This time the badges are made of fake leather and are square,

subtitles.original: 変わっておりますが、もちろんこれでも痛バッグとかできたりしますよ。
subtitles.english: which is unusual, but of course you can still make an ita-bag with them.

subtitles.original: 最後のアイテムは、KFPチキンをモデルにしたスマートフォンリングです。
subtitles.english: Last item is the smartphone ring, modeled after KFP chickens.

subtitles.original: これがあれば、羽でスマホを持つのもそんなに難しくなくなるかも？可愛い！
subtitles.english: Maybe this way, holding your phones won't be as hard with your wings? Cute!

subtitles.original: 最後のアイテムはスマホリング！もちろんKFPモチーフです。
subtitles.english: The last item is a phone ring! Of course, it's a KFP motif.

subtitles.original: そしてそれ以外にも、ボイスパックがあります。
subtitles.english: And besides that, there's also a voice pack.

subtitles.original: ここ数ヶ月ボイスパックを出していなかったので、ぜひ手に入れてくださいね。激レアです！
subtitles.english: I haven't been doing any voice packs these past months, so make sure to get this one. Rare!

subtitles.original: そのほかには、久しぶりにボイスもあります。
subtitles.english: Other than that, there's also a voice pack after a long time.

subtitles.original: 最近あんまり参加してないので、ぜひこの機会に、おねがいします！
subtitles.english: I haven't done many lately, so please take this opportunity!

responses.thoughts:
**Subtitle Generation Synthesis**

Alright, let's break this down. My task is to synchronize subtitles, both original (OG) and English (EN), for a video of Takanashi Kiara singing and presenting her birthday merchandise. The core of this operation lies in meticulous transcription driven by audio, with specific constraints: vocal events are the only start triggers. Visual elements are exceptions. We're using a decoding hierarchy, adherence to precise timing (MM:SS.mmm format, truncation rules, no instantaneous transitions). Line length is limited to 50 characters, and context-aware translations are a must. No Closed Captioning tags are allowed. Importantly, the output needs to be a valid JSON object formatted in a markdown block with `global_analysis` at the top.

So, first thing's first, I dive into the music segment (00:00 - 02:32). I start with a very close listen to pick up the lyrics, paying special attention to the exact timing of each phrase. We can hear the song, but it feels like it starts mid chorus. That's fine, I check the lyrics to verify. Crowd hype-calls such as "Oi!" "Wow wow yeah!" may be included, but only if they are distinct and don't muddy the core lyrics. For the verses, I meticulously check the lyrics to catch the original, as I am intimately familiar with her music. The same goes for the rest of the song, making sure to track the instrumentals and confetti gun timing.

Then, there's the birthday merchandise presentation (02:58 - end). Here I need to carefully transcribe both the English and Japanese dialogue. It's a combination of direct transcription and context-aware translation. Every line is carefully timed and translated with accuracy. I have to match the translations to the Japanese and make sure everything is perfect and accurate.

Finally, I refine the timecodes and polish the translations to make them sound natural and fluid. I'm focusing on ensuring accuracy with the timecodes and line splits. Once everything is refined, I double-check to make sure all the parameters are met: output format, the timing, any disallowed features. Also, confirm the JSON format and structure. It needs to be perfect. After the double check, the only thing that's left is to export the final file!
